vehicles.slot: Slot at base of gear selector on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Slot at base of gear selector on center console.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Battery is under the rear seat — but use the jump terminals UNDER THE HOOD to connect your jump pack. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Slot at base of gear selector on center console.
vehicles.notes: Manual: depress clutch, select N — no shift lock.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: RWD: Lift from REAR. Rear (drive) wheels are lifted in the air. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

battery.location: In trunk (right side under panel) or under rear seat
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red fused terminal near firewall (+), black ground bolt on strut tower (−)
battery.warnings: Always use hood terminals — never jump directly at battery. AGM rated jump pack.

vehicles.slot: Near base of gear selector on console — trim piece may need removal
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock slot — Near base of gear selector on console — trim piece may need removal.
Insert a thin key or small flathead into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
Press the brake pedal while holding the slot.
Move the ZF 8HP selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — In trunk right side under panel. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
BMW/ZF 8HP: battery is typically NOT under the hood — in trunk right side under panel. Always use the hood jump terminals.
vehicles.notes: ZF 8HP is reliable. Manual: clutch to N.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. xDrive AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

battery.location: In trunk right side under panel
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red fused terminal (+), black ground bolt (−)
battery.warnings: Use hood terminals only. AGM jump pack.

vehicles.slot: Trim cap on console near selector — pry to expose slot
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Trim cap on console near selector — pry to expose slot.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, driver side (no trunk battery on E46).
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Trim cap on console near selector — pry to expose slot.
vehicles.notes: Use hood jump terminals — no need to access trunk battery directly.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. xDrive AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

battery.location: Under hood, driver side (no trunk battery on E46)
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals, easy access
battery.warnings: E46 is one of the few BMWs with a conventional under-hood battery.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot under trim near base
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Electronic selector — slot under trim near base.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — In trunk under panel. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.

vehicles.notes: M5 E60 uses SMG — REQUIRES POWER to shift to neutral. Jump battery FIRST on M5.
vehicles.warn: M5 E60 SMG: jump battery first. No mechanical override on SMG.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. xDrive AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

knownIssues.title: M5 E60 SMG Has No Mechanical Neutral Override
knownIssues.detail: The M5 E60 uses a Single Mono-clutch Gearbox (SMG) that is fully electronic. There is NO mechanical override to shift to neutral. The 12V battery MUST be jumped first before any shift to neutral is possible. Flatbed is the only safe option with a completely dead battery.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red fused terminal (+), black ground bolt (−)
battery.warnings: Use hood terminals. M5 E60 SMG: must restore power before any neutral shift — no mechanical override.

vehicles.slot: Base of selector COLUMN — small slot under a cap (iDrive electronic selector)
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle uses a COLUMN-MOUNTED shifter — the shift lock slot is on the STEERING COLUMN, not the floor console.
Locate the slot on the steering column — Base of selector COLUMN — small slot under a cap (iDrive electronic selector).
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot on the column.
Press firmly while pressing the brake pedal.
Move the column shifter to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Battery is under the rear seat — but use the jump terminals UNDER THE HOOD to connect your jump pack. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and move column shifter to N normally.
vehicles.notes: iDrive selector — slot is at column base, not console. Air suspension — check clearance.
vehicles.warn: Air suspension: verify clearance before flatbed.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. xDrive AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: AIR SUSPENSION: May lower/raise unexpectedly. Verify ride height is stable before loading on flatbed. Some models have a 'jack mode' or 'transport mode' — check with owner if possible.

knownIssues.title: Air Suspension Clearance Risk
knownIssues.detail: Air suspension can drop to bump stops when power is lost, making flatbed loading difficult or damaging the undercarriage. If possible, restore 12V power to raise suspension before loading. Check clearance carefully.

knownIssues.title: iDrive Electronic Shifter
knownIssues.detail: This generation uses an electronic column-mounted iDrive selector. The shift lock slot is at the base of the selector column, not on the center console. Operators unfamiliar with this layout often cannot locate it.

battery.location: Under rear seat or in trunk — varies
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red fused terminal (+), black ground bolt (−)
battery.warnings: Use hood terminals only. Air suspension drops with dead battery. AGM jump pack.

vehicles.slot: Slot near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Slot near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Slot near gear selector base on center console.
vehicles.notes: All E83 X3 are xDrive AWD.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — xDrive AWD is full-time, cannot be disabled. If no flatbed available, use wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: FULL-TIME AWD: Cannot disengage AWD. Flatbed is the safest method. If using wheel-lift, dollies MUST be used on the free-rolling axle to prevent driveline damage.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals

vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — xDrive AWD is full-time, cannot be disabled. If no flatbed available, use wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FULL-TIME AWD: Cannot disengage AWD. Flatbed is the safest method. If using wheel-lift, dollies MUST be used on the free-rolling axle to prevent driveline damage.
AIR SUSPENSION: May lower/raise unexpectedly. Verify ride height is stable before loading on flatbed. Some models have a 'jack mode' or 'transport mode' — check with owner if possible.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals
battery.warnings: AGM jump pack. PHEV: 12V only.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot under trim near selector
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock slot — Electronic selector — slot under trim near selector.
Insert a thin key or small flathead into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
Press the brake pedal while holding the slot.
Move the ZF 8HP selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
BMW/ZF 8HP: battery is typically NOT under the hood — under hood. Always use the hood jump terminals.

towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.

AIR SUSPENSION: May lower/raise unexpectedly. Verify ride height is stable before loading on flatbed. Some models have a 'jack mode' or 'transport mode' — check with owner if possible.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals
battery.warnings: AGM jump pack. PHEV: 12V only.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot under trim near selector base
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock slot — Electronic selector — slot under trim near selector base.
Insert a thin key or small flathead into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
Press the brake pedal while holding the slot.
Move the ZF 8HP selector to Neutral.

Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
BMW/ZF 8HP: battery is typically NOT under the hood — under hood. Always use the hood jump terminals.

towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FULL-TIME AWD: Cannot disengage AWD. Flatbed is the safest method. If using wheel-lift, dollies MUST be used on the free-rolling axle to prevent driveline damage.
AIR SUSPENSION: May lower/raise unexpectedly. Verify ride height is stable before loading on flatbed. Some models have a 'jack mode' or 'transport mode' — check with owner if possible.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals
battery.warnings: AGM jump pack. PHEV: 12V only.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot under trim at selector base
shift: Turn the BMW iX / i4 / i5 / i7 to ON/Ready mode.
Shift lock slot: Electronic selector — slot under trim at selector base.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly.
Press the brake pedal while holding the slot.
Move selector to N.
FLATBED ONLY — do not tow with driven wheels on the ground.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Open the trunk — battery is ix: under hood. i4/i5/i7: in trunk. Connect jump pack. 12V AUXILIARY BATTERY ONLY — never touch the high-voltage battery pack.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
FLATBED ONLY.
vehicles.warn: FLATBED ONLY. Never tow with driven wheels on ground.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — electric drivetrain
towNotes: ELECTRIC VEHICLE: FLATBED ONLY — never tow with any driven wheels on the ground. Regenerative braking system can damage motor/inverter.
If vehicle must be moved short distance without flatbed, use dollies under ALL driven wheels.
12V battery ONLY for jumping — NEVER touch or access the high-voltage (HV) system. HV components are marked with ORANGE cables/covers.

knownIssues.title: FLATBED ONLY — High Voltage System
knownIssues.detail: 800V high-voltage system. Never attempt to tow with wheels on ground. Never connect jumper cables to HV components. 12V auxiliary battery only for powering electronics.

knownIssues.title: Battery Location Varies by Model
knownIssues.detail: iX: 12V under hood. i4/i5/i7: 12V in trunk. Always verify before attempting to jump.

battery.location: iX: under hood. i4/i5/i7: in trunk
battery.jumpTerminals: See location. 12V auxiliary terminals only.
battery.warnings: NEVER touch HV orange cables. 12V auxiliary only for all jump operations.

vehicles.slot: Slot near gear selector base
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Slot near gear selector base.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — In trunk, right side under panel.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Slot near gear selector base.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: RWD: Lift from REAR. Rear (drive) wheels are lifted in the air. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
LOW CLEARANCE VEHICLE: Use flatbed. Check ground clearance before attempting any wheel-lift — front splitter/rear diffuser damage is common.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

battery.location: In trunk, right side under panel
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red fused terminal (+), black ground bolt (−)
battery.warnings: Use hood terminals.

vehicles.slot: Slot at selector base — trim piece may need removal
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Slot at selector base — trim piece may need removal.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — In trunk, right side under panel. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Slot at selector base — trim piece may need removal.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: RWD: Lift from REAR. Rear (drive) wheels are lifted in the air. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
LOW CLEARANCE VEHICLE: Use flatbed. Check ground clearance before attempting any wheel-lift — front splitter/rear diffuser damage is common.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

battery.location: In trunk, right side under panel
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red fused terminal (+), black ground bolt (−)
battery.warnings: Use hood terminals.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot under trim near base
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Electronic selector — slot under trim near base.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — In trunk, under panel. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: RWD: Lift from REAR. Rear (drive) wheels are lifted in the air. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
LOW CLEARANCE VEHICLE: Use flatbed. Check ground clearance before attempting any wheel-lift — front splitter/rear diffuser damage is common.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red fused terminal (+), black ground bolt (−)
battery.warnings: Use hood terminals.
